title: A new rhythm rises from the Atlantic coast
dek: Dakar’s newest sound is local, electronic and uninterested in fitting anyone else’s category.
author: Fatou Diop
body: After midnight, a converted warehouse in Dakar begins to pulse with a rhythm that combines sabar percussion, field recordings and spare electronic bass.
The artists shaping the scene draw freely from the city around them. They release music online, collaborate across languages and build their own visual identities.
What sounds new is also a continuation: another generation using Dakar’s openness to make music that belongs to a precise place and travels far beyond it.
